payload.title: AWS Security LIVE! | November 27, 2024
payload.description: Hosted by security experts, AWS Security LIVE! showcases AWS Partners tackling real-world security challenges. Join live and get your security questions answered.
payload.body_html:
AWS Security LIVE!

Hosted by security experts, AWS Security LIVE! showcases AWS Partners tackling real-world security challenges. Join live and get your security questions answered.

More info about AWS Security LIVE!

AWS

Articles written by current and past AWS Developer Advocates to help people interested in building on AWS. Opinions are each author's own.

payload.title: Now
payload.body_html:
The Best Time is Now

One of the most common things we hear developers say they wished they were doing more of, is writing about their work and sharing it with others. dev.to is fundamentally built to help facilitate that process. It's well established that writing and sharing your work is just about the best thing you can do to help your own understanding and boost your career prospects.

As the saying goes, "The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now." In order to help you take the leap, we've created some templates for your first DEV post. You're welcome to post about anything related to software development, but you may want to start with one of these templates.

Simply click one of these blue buttons to launch a ready-made template in our new article editor. Have fun!

Today I Learned What new concept have you learned recently? (it doesn't have to be today) There may be some other devs out there who could benefit from a bit of knowledge share. Here's an example

X In 30 Seconds Flex your ability to explain something simply and briefly. Can you cover a topic in only one paragraph? Give it a shot. Here's an example.

Letter to My Past Self Now that you've made some progress in your journey, what realization have you made that could have saved you from some struggle earlier in your career? Here's an example

The dev.to editor uses Markdown syntax. Here's the guide. Thanks so much for being a caring member of the DEV community. We're all in this together.

❤️

payload.title: Community and Campus Leaders Guide
payload.description: How to use DEV if you're a community leader.
payload.body_markdown:
First of all, thank you for being leaders and advocates in your community! Community is vital to the ever-evolving software development career, and your role as a leader and connector is especially important in making the magic happen. We know that community-building takes a lot of time and dedication, so we’re here to help.

DEV is an open-source platform where programmers share knowledge and support one another. We make it easy for you to stay connected to your friends, classmates, and colleagues as you collaborate or reflect on assignments, projects and events.

As a leader, you can use DEV to build a space that elevates and connects members of your school and organization together. Here’s what you get by connecting your community through DEV: 

- An organization page that highlights the people in your group, their articles, and their discussions.
- Organization branding on all published articles and discussions from your members.
- DEV Connect: a dedicated chat room with collaboration tools for everyone in your organization to stay connected. (Video chat coming soon!)

As organization members, individuals can:

- Show off their coding projects (we support pretty embeds from GitHub, repl.it, CodePen, and more!)
- Document their process, progress, and learnings
- Start discussions
- Support one another in DEV Connect: proofread articles, solve coding problems, or simply say hi.

In addition to staying connected with one another, your community will also naturally be connected with the greater developer community!

### How To Get Started

1. Create a [DEV](https://dev.to) account.
2. Create an [organization account](https://dev.to/settings/organization) on DEV.
3. Customize your organization page to your heart’s content
4. Tell everyone in your group to create a DEV account
5. Send them your organization key
6. Once they receive the key, tell them to visit their organization page. There will be a prompt for them to join an organization by submitting their key.
7. Say hello to your members in your dedicated [DEV Connect](https://dev.to/connect) chat channel. New members of your organization are automatically added to the chat channels.

That’s it! You should start seeing familiar faces pop up on your organization page :)

### Publishing Under Your Organization

Once you or someone from your organization is ready to publish an article, all they have to do is visit https://dev.to/new, write their article, and select your organization from the dropdown menu at the top of the page. It’s that simple!

As an administrator, you can see all articles published under your organization by visiting your [dashboard](https://dev.to/dashboard). From there, you can:

- See article statistics
- Edit the article
- Set the experience level of each article
- Re-assign the article authorship to someone else in the organization

### Tip & Tricks

Here are some general tips and tricks to enhance your DEV experience:

- Read our [editor guide](https://dev.to/p/editor_guide)! We have lots of cool ways to embed content within your article.
- Encourage your community to leave comments on each other’s articles. This always makes the author feel special.
- [Open up your inbox](https://dev.to/settings/misc)! This way, your community members can message you directly.
- Let your community know that they can [hide comments](https://dev.to/devteam/changelog-hidden-comments-53l9) or [report abuse](https://dev.to/report-abuse) easily. We take your safety seriously!

That was a lot of words, but we swear it’ll all come naturally once you sign up. If you have any questions or suggested, please email us at scrubbed@example.com -- we love hearing from our community leaders.
